nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Egmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Esmond (Old English Ēastmund, ēast ‘grace, beauty’ + mund ‘protection’). A Norman French cognate, of ancient Germanic origin, is also found. See also Eastman .

variants.name: Eastman
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Estmund (Old English Ēastmund, from ēast ‘east’ + mund ‘protection’). See also Esmond .
English: occasionally a variant of Heasman, a topographic name for a dweller ‘(in the) brushwood’, from Old English hǣse ‘brushwood’ + mann (compare Hayes 3).
Americanized form (translation into English) of Swedish Östman (see Ostman ) and North German Ostmann or Östmann (see Oestmann ).

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Willem I van Egmond was born in 1180, in Binnenmaas, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond, was 35 and his mother, Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre, was 20. He had at least 1 son with Badeloch Clementina van Amstel. He died on 17 May 1234, in Schweiz, Zwickau, Saxony, Germany, at the age of 54, and was buried in North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem I van Egmond est né en 1180, à Binnenmaas, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Badeloch Clementina van Amstel. Il est décédé le 17 mai 1234, à Schweiz, Zwickau, Sachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.Egmond aan den Hoef, North Holland, Neth: Egmond aan den Hoef, Bergen, Nord-Holland, Niederlande
de.lifeSummary: Als Willem I van Egmond 1180 in Binnenmaas, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond 35 und seine Mutter Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre 20. Er hatte mit Badeloch Clementina van Amstel mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 17. Mai 1234 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Schweiz, Zwickau, Sachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Badeloch Clementina van Amstel. Morì il 17 maggio 1234, nel Schweiz, Zwickau, Sachsen, Germania, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ko.lifeSummary: Willem I van Egmond(이)가 1180년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 빈마아스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Badeloch Clementina van Amstel(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1234년 5월 17일 독일 작센 즈윅오 슈위즈에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Egmond nasceu em 1180, em Binnenmaas, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Badeloch Clementina van Amstel. Ele faleceu em 17 de maio de 1234, em Schweiz, Zwickau, Sachsen, Alemanha, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem I van Egmond родился в 1180 г. в местности бинненмаас, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Wouter I Allardszn van Egmond было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Mabilia I van IJsselmonde van Gelre, 20. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Badeloch Clementina van Amstel. Он умер 17 мая 1234 г. в местности щвиз, звиккау, сачсен, Германия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.
